string.text: Tell me about yourself.
Why the hell would I? You're mistaking me for someone else, stalker. I've been walking this land for a long time and I know better than to let anyone into my pocket or my soul.
You got any work for me?

Of course! Listen, our gatherers told me that mutants are bothering them, making it hard to collect artifacts and all that. Your job is to deal with those mutants — it's your specialty after all, hunter! As a reward for the cleanup, I’ll give you a good artifact. You’ll find it useful, trust me!
Alright, I’m in.
Maybe another time.
Who are you, stalker?! How can I ever thank you?
They call me Snake. And you must be Zhekan, right?
Yeah, but... How do you know me?
Get up. Once we’re outside, you’ll understand.
That’s it then — finally some justice. I almost forgot what it feels like! Thank you so much, Snake!
Always happy to help. I think we shouldn’t stick around here for too long, don’t you agree?
You’re right. Let’s head back to camp. By the way, I’ve got something for you there — you’ve earned it!
Alright, let’s go!
I think now’s the right time to attack.
You’re right. I’ll call the guys and we’ll move out. Are you ready?
Ready.
Hold on, I need to grab something.
So Zhekan is still alive — that’s already good news. I think we need to try and get him out of the bandits’ captivity. Are you with us?
I’ve already dug this deep, would be stupid to back out now.
Glad to hear it. Come see me at night and we’ll head out.
Alright.
Here, take your "gadget".
Oh! You found it — good job. I hope that wasn’t the only thing in the stash?
Not just that, there was also the old man’s manuscript. It helped me figure out where the entrance to the catacombs is. By the way, what’s in the case?
Snake, I don’t meddle in your business, right? So you shouldn’t meddle in mine. Thanks for the job — you're dismissed.
Alright, take care.
I don’t mean to push, but didn’t you mention a “gift” earlier?

Yeah, yeah! I remember. Here, take it. This is a very good and rare suit — it used to belong to one of the now-defunct groups, "Clear Sky". You might’ve heard of them. Anyway, these suits were custom made by Novikov himself. As he used to say: "We'll duct tape the hell out of everything with blue tape!" So yeah — take care of it.
Thanks a ton, Rzhavy! I was expecting a gun, but this — a suit from a defunct faction? That’s something else! Thanks again!
Before you die, I want to know — what did you do to Zhekan?
Funny how it turned out... Some random hunter managed to dig up more info than the guys who were right there the whole time. Didn’t expect that…
So you even teamed up with the bandits... Scum! Alright then, see you in the afterlife…
Listen, I don’t care about you. I only care about the information on Zhekan.
I’m afraid it’s already too late…
You sure about that? You can die quickly, or die slowly. I can make that happen!
So bloodthirsty? I don’t think so…
Wanna find out?
Alright, damn it, I’ll talk…
That’s more like it…
Zhekan is still alive, he's being held captive by the bandits from the village. They’ve got him in the basement — that’s all I know. Though… he might not be alive anymore.
Hello, tell me about the stalker named Nazar.
Nazar? Not much to say. He was an experienced stalker — a natural-born scavenger, you could say. Why do you ask?
Just found his body. And his PDA.
So what? I still don’t get what you want!
Nothing much, just curious — when did you start working with the “Blues”?
What do you mean? I don’t work with anyone! You drunk or what?!
You don’t? Well, okay then…
Snake, I told the guys about Costa’s schemes. They agree with me — they want blood vengeance too.
Alright, but let’s handle this outside the camp. And before we kill him, I want to interrogate him.
Alright. Shall we go?
Yeah, let’s go.
Rzhavy, we’ve got a situation…
Well? Spit it out.
Well?
Son of a bitch! He just sold him out… I bet Nazar isn’t the first one who ended up dead because of his tips. By the way, you didn’t get to meet our leader — Zhekan, did you?
No, why?

A week ago, Zhekan went on a raid with Costa. Never came back. Our "buddy" Costa said he went missing, but not before naming him his second-in-command. We were suspicious, sure, but this is the Zone — weird stuff happens, so we believed him. Now I think he killed Zhekan, just to take his place.
So what are we going to do?
I think a public execution would be the best course of action.
I agree. But before we do that, we need to find out what he did to Zhekan, and where he might be.
Alright, I’ll call for you in a couple of hours. I’m going to tell the guys, so no one’s left in the dark.
I’ll be waiting.
Anyway, remember how you asked me to look into what the hell the “Blues” were doing around here?
Yeah, I remember, so what? You already told me — said they were looking for some kind of artifact.
They were… But the artifact is nothing compared to what else I managed to dig up.
What is it? Out with it already!

When I was tailing the “Blues,” I took out one of their couriers — he had a PDA. At first, I didn’t think much of it. But after I reported everything to you, I went to Ivanich and asked him to unlock the PDA. He did, and there was a lead in there — turns out Costa is somehow connected to the “Blues.” So I waited until nightfall and headed to their camp. Thought they were gone already, but they were waiting for me. And they were waiting because Costa warned them. I’ll forward the proof to your PDA right now.
Costa? Mercenaries?... Damn, I mean, I noticed he wasn’t too chatty when it came to the mercs, but working with them?
Read what I just sent you.
[Reads message archive from Krivoy’s PDA.]
Tell me about yourself.

I’m what you young folks call “an old Soviet.” I’m from the Donetsk region, got my degree, moved to the city as a young specialist to work at a factory — figured I’d spend my life there. I’m an engineer. I ended up here by accident — got stiffed on a job, no pay. Then I met a guy back on the "Big Land," he told me about the Zone, said you could make some good money here. So I came. I’ve seen all sorts of things since then, but decided to settle down and fix things for the guys — good thing I’ve got both the skills and the hands for it.
Got your message — well? Did you manage to crack it?
Of course I did! Here’s the device — and as a proper technician, I didn’t go poking around inside. All the info’s yours — knock yourself out!
Ivanich, I need your help with something!
Something, huh? Alright, spill it — unless you’re messing around.
I need a PDA unlocked — can you help?
I can help — but not for free, obviously.
How much?
Let’s say fifteen hundred, and we’ll part ways like ships in the night.
Alright, here’s the PDA.
I don’t have that kind of money.
Great! I’ll get in touch with you as soon as I’m done.
Come back when you’ve got it, then.
Here are the maps you mentioned.
Good job, Snake! Here's your reward.
How are you feeling after being held captive?
Better now. Listen — while I was in captivity, I overheard the bandits talking about a downed helicopter somewhere nearby. Supposedly, there’s a map of the Zone in it with all sorts of markings.
So what do you want?
I want you to find that helicopter and retrieve the maps. I’ll pay you well. You should start looking near the transport tunnel.
Alright, I’ll try to find it.
Sorry, but I’ve got too much on my plate right now…
Got your message — what’s going on?
It’s… well… They found the old man — not exactly in one piece…
Goddamn it! So what now?!

Hold on! His PDA had info about some stash — here are the coordinates. There might be something in there that could help you. I’ll need one gadget from that stash, though. You’ll know what I mean when you find it.
Deal.
What can you tell me about this place?

Well, listen: We’re on the grounds of what used to be a research institute. Some say it was here even before the Chernobyl disaster. After the accident, the military set up shop here. What were they doing? No idea. But there’s plenty of evidence they were here — just step outside and all the military tech will catch your eye. Northeast of us, there are some abandoned hangars — looked like a repair base or maybe just a bus depot. Hard to tell. But there’s one detail — in one of those hangars, there’s an entrance to some underground facility. What’s down there? No one knows. No one’s ever managed to get the doors open. A bit further from the hangars, there’s an old military base, but it’s sealed off and crawling with anomalies — I wouldn’t recommend going there. To the east, there’s a transport tunnel — our guys are stationed there, but bandits often raid the place. Beyond that is an old checkpoint, and that’s bandit territory now — they either shoot or rob anyone passing through…
Fascinating stuff!

Hold on! That’s not all! To the southwest, there’s an old village — that’s where our “younger brothers” live. Nearby, there are a couple of anomalous clusters. Further southeast, there’s a small camp of our brothers, and a road as well — though that one’s littered with anomalies.
Wait, are you saying there’s no way out of here?
What do you mean, no way out? There is one — you got here, didn’t you? That’s why we don’t lose hope. Guides know about secret paths we’d never find on our own! That’s how it is.
Tell me about yourself?

Haven’t been asked that in a while… Feels strange, hmm. Not much to tell really — just a regular stalker like everyone else here. Started out running errands for Sidorovich in the Cordon. Then I joined a group of loners doing artifact hunting on commission. They taught me the ropes, and then I started going solo. Got tired of it quickly though. I don’t have any problems in the Big Land, but I came to the Zone out of curiosity — and there’s just something about this place that keeps you from getting bored.
So how did you end up here?

It’s simple. A group of stalkers led by Jekan decided to explore new areas. I joined them, and here we are. Jekan was a strange guy, but a great leader. Now, where he is or whether he’ll come back — no one knows. Before he left, he made me his second-in-command and went on a raid. Why or for how long — nobody ever found out.
Oh look, it’s the rat who killed one of ours. Well, pal — mind telling me what the hell you’re doing here?!
[Remains silent]
Playing the silent game, huh? We’ve got all day. My boys and I aren’t in a hurry, so we’ll just wait until you feel like talking.
[Remains silent]
Snake? What are you doing here?
Oh, just decided to catch some sun. You?
Us? Same old — got orders to secure the area, and some intel on a “blue” camp.
Got it. Alright, Lieutenant, I’ll be going.
Lieutenant, I’ve got a question.
I’m listening.
You didn’t happen to find any keys on those “blue” guys, did you?
I did find one — but I’m not giving it away just like that…
I don’t have much money, and as you can see — I’ve got no gear at all.

Relax, always straight to material stuff… I don’t need any of that. There’s only one thing I’m interested in: why were you locked up in the “Blues” detention cell? Snake, I’ve known you a long time — don’t try to dodge or spin tales.

A stalker named Rusty tipped me off. He was concerned about the “Blues” showing up in this area and asked me to dig up more info. So I did. I found out the “Blues” had some kind of connection with the local neutral leader. I learned that from one of their PDAs. Turns out the “Blues” had been commissioned to get a rare artifact. They didn’t want to find it themselves and hired a local scavenger. The guy found it — but instead of paying him, they just offed him. And since no one in the camp talks about this scavenger, I figured the leader hushed it up. That’s why I decided to gather more evidence and corner the rat. But instead of finding clues, I got knocked out… I think they were expecting me.

You’re probably right! Someone must’ve tipped them off — which means someone else didn’t want you sniffing around. By the way, there was a message archive on the “Blue’s” PDA. Might be of interest to you. I sent it your way.
Thanks, Lieutenant. Take care.
Lieutenant, got a question for you.
I'm listening.
You didn’t happen to find any keys on the “Blue” guys, did you?
I found one — but I’m not giving it up for nothing…
I don’t have much money, and I’m completely out of gear as you can see.
Calm down — always about the loot… I don’t want any of that. I only want to know one thing: why were you in the “Blues” holding cell? Snake, I’ve known you for a long time — skip the stories.

Looks like the “Blues” bribed the local neutral leader, who then sold them an experienced artifact scavenger — Nazar. That stalker found a rare artifact for them on request. But instead of paying him, they just killed him — no loose ends. I found Nazar’s body and decided to gather more evidence of the “Blues” working with the local neutral leader. Came back here at night to investigate, but instead of clues, someone cracked me over the head. Just like that…

Yeah… Sad to hear that neutrals are selling out their own… Alright, here’s the key. Oh, and Snake — in the “Blue’s” PDA, there was a chat log. Might’ve been with the leader of those neutrals. I already sent you the archive.
Thanks, Lieutenant. I won’t forget it!
I think you’ll be interested in this...
What do you have?
Nazar’s PDA.
I won’t even ask where you got that — I’ll give you 1500.
Deal.
Rest in peace, Nazar...
PDA of a mercenary envoy. Found it near the military base.
I’ll give you 1000 for it.
Deal.
Very interesting… Nicely done, hunter!
Laptop from the mercenary camp.
I’ll offer 2000 for it.
Deal.
So that’s how it is! Interesting… Very interesting!
Stalker Rezac’s PDA.
I’ll offer 500 for it.
Deal.
Just as I thought — nothing interesting...
Tell me about yourself.
Honestly, I don’t even know what to say… Just a regular stalker, not a vet but no rookie either. Came to the Zone a couple years ago, don’t really know many people. That’s about it.
The mutant’s dead — tell me what you know about Bison’s group.
They were here, but didn’t stay long. Stocked up on food and ammo, then left. Heard from their leader they were heading toward the tunnel — that’s where you’ll find them. That’s all I know.
Hmm… Not much, but thanks anyway.
Looks like you wanted something?
Exactly! I won’t beat around the bush — I need information about the catacombs.
What catacombs?

Damn... So, I went to the tunnel you told me about. Didn’t find the group there, but I did find traces of their presence and a PDA belonging to one of them. The PDA mentions some catacombs that would lead the group somewhere, but unfortunately, I couldn’t get more info. That’s why I’ve come back to you for help.

Interesting... I’ve heard of the catacombs, sure, but I always thought they were just stories. Turns out they’re not. As for the catacombs themselves, I can’t help you, but there’s an old man who sometimes drops by the bar. He’s around here pretty often, and he should know what these catacombs are and where they lead.
Great. Where can I find him?
That I don’t know. But I can let you know when he shows up again — not for free, of course.
Of course. What do you want in return?

Here’s the deal — I don’t have any jobs right now to settle the score. So I suggest this: when something comes up, you handle it. Deal? By the way, if you don’t want to wait for my message, ask around — maybe the guys have some easy work for you.
Deal.
A hunter? Interesting! Very interesting... Why are you standing there? Speak up — what brings you here?
How do you know I’m a hunter? Eh, never mind. Costa said I should talk to you about Bison’s group. What can you tell me?
About Bison’s group? Not much, but you understand you’ll have to pay for the info, right?
I understand. How much?
No, no, no — you misunderstood. I’m not really interested in money. I have a more productive offer.
I’m listening...
Alright, there’s a swamp not far from here, and some creature lives there that’s been interfering with our guys collecting artifacts. Sounds like a job right up your alley.
Let’s get this straight. I kill the creature, and you tell me everything you know about Bison’s group. That right?
That’s right. Look, here’s where the creature is. Once you’re done, come back.
Alright, wait for me.
New face? Interesting! So, who are you? What brings you here?
I’m a hunter — they call me Snake, and I’ve got business. I’m looking for a group of stalkers led by Bison. According to my info, they should have passed through here. What can you tell me?

Well then, Hunter Snake... honestly, I’ve got nothing to tell you. You’d better head to the bar and talk to Foma, our local trader. If your people were here, they definitely talked to him. By the way, they call me Kosta, just so you know.
Thanks, I’ll go have a word with him.
Hey, stalker! I know you!
Dude, you must be mistaken. It’s my first time here — no way you know me...
Oh no, I remember! You’re the hunter — Snake, if I’m not mistaken. You were with Gonta’s group, clearing out that snork nest near Zaton, right?
Yeah... that happened. Why?
No reason — I was the stalker you guys saved. They call me Rusty, by the way. Nice to meet you.
Ah, I see! That clears things up. Nice to meet you too. So, have you been around here long?
Not really — just started my third week. Why?
Listen, have you seen Bison’s group or the man himself by any chance?
Nope, man. Better go check with our leader or the trader — if they were here, those guys would know for sure.
Alright, I’ll talk to them. Anyway, I gotta go…
So, what’s the deal with the “blue boys”?

Nothing extraordinary. They were hired to search for a rare artifact, but I couldn't find out which one. The important thing I learned is that they plan to leave soon. So, no need to worry about them anymore.
Is that all you found out?
They didn’t look for the artifact themselves, they hired some stalker. That guy found the artifact, and they killed him. Also, their leader, a guy called Crooked, that’s all.
Not much, but let’s hope they leave. Anyway, I’ll try to dig up some information about the leader of the “blues.”
Try, but something tells me they’re just a one-off group. They were hired, don’t know each other, and once the job’s done, they’ll scatter. By the way, what about my reward?
Who knows… Here’s your reward, I think it’s more than enough!
Well, here you are. Want to make some money?
Of course, tell me what needs to be done.

Look, our guys just came back from a raid and said they saw a small group of mercenaries. They were hanging around the old military base. The guys waited a bit and saw how the “blues” easily broke the gate and disappeared inside. Your job is to find out what the hell the “blues” are doing here.

"Blues"? They’re everywhere, with contracts all over the Zone, so it’s not surprising they showed up here. By the way, did you tell Kosta about the “blues”? And why do you care what the “blues” are doing here?

I did tell him, but he just shrugged and said it’s none of our business and we shouldn’t interfere with the “blues.” I wouldn’t bother, but something about their presence near our camp is bugging me. So, what do you think?
Kosta’s right, it’s best not to get involved with the “blues,” but on the other hand… Alright, fine. I hope the reward’s worth it.
You won’t be disappointed with the reward, don’t worry!
I hope so… Because I’ll be putting myself in danger.
Ivanych, I’ve got news about the scanner and the lockpick.
What’s the news?
The lockpick’s dead, probably killed by mutants. The scanner was with him, and I’ve installed it at the spot you mentioned.
Pity about the stalker, but this is the Zone — no one’s safe from anything...
I agree. Do you have any other work for me?
Yeah, there is. Come back later, I’m a bit busy right now.
Alright, see you.
Good luck, do you have any work for me?
Thank you! Work? Always. It all depends on what you're good at...

What am I good at? A lot of things. For example, I have experience working with the scientists' techs. I helped them set up equipment and measure anomalous fields. I'm also quite good at hunting all kinds of wildlife.
Really? Hunting doesn’t interest me much, but your experience with scientists is just what I need.
Well… Don't keep me in suspense. What’s the job?

Well, I signed a contract with the scientists a couple of months ago. They provide me with equipment, and I set up scanners or find people for research in various complexes, basically like a foreman — make a little money for myself and for the people. So, regarding the scanners, I sent a stalker a while ago to install one, but I haven’t heard from him in ages. The last time he contacted me, it was near the anomaly where the scanner should be installed. I have a feeling he’s dead, but the job needs to be done. So, find him and grab the scanner from him. I'll mark the installation point for you, and don’t worry about the reward.
Got it, I’ll head out right away.
